overview.whyItMatters: GenAI latency directly impacts user experience. A 5-second response feels slow; 10 seconds feels broken. Understanding performance optimization—from model selection to retrieval tuning to streaming—is essential for production applications.

concepts.title: Latency Optimization Strategies
concepts.content:
Latency in GenAI comes from multiple sources: network, retrieval, model inference, and post-processing. Optimize each component.

**Pre-Computation:**
For predictable queries, pre-compute responses. Nightly batch jobs generate answers to common questions. Real-time requests retrieve pre-computed results. Works for stable content like FAQs.

**Bedrock Latency-Optimized Models:**
Some models offer latency-optimized variants. Trade some quality for faster response. Choose based on your latency requirements and quality tolerance.

**Parallel Requests:**
When you need multiple model calls, run them in parallel. Step Functions parallel states. Concurrent Lambda invocations. Don't serialize independent operations.

**Streaming:**
Stream responses to users. First token appears quickly even if full response takes time. Dramatically improves perceived latency. Users see progress instead of waiting.

**Geographic Distribution:**
CloudFront serves from edge locations. For global users, reduce network latency by caching at the edge. Consider Cross-Region Inference for Bedrock to route to closest region.
keyPoints: Use latency-optimized model variants when available
Stream responses to improve perceived latency
concepts.examTip: 'Reduce latency' -> streaming, pre-computation, parallel requests, latency-optimized models.

concepts.title: Retrieval System Performance
concepts.content:
RAG systems spend significant time in retrieval. Optimize vector search and document retrieval for overall performance.

**Index Optimization:**
OpenSearch index settings impact search performance. Shard count, replica count, refresh intervals. Right-size for your workload: too few shards causes hot spots, too many causes overhead.

**Query Preprocessing:**
Optimize queries before search. Expand queries for better recall. Remove noise words. Rewrite for the index's expectations. Good preprocessing improves both quality and speed.

**Hybrid Search Configuration:**
Combine keyword (BM25) and vector (semantic) search. Configure weighting between methods. Hybrid often gives best results, but tuning matters. Custom scoring functions can prioritize based on your requirements.

**Bedrock Reranker Models:**
Rerank retrieved results for relevance. Initial retrieval returns candidates; reranker reorders by relevance. Adds latency but improves quality. Use when retrieval precision matters more than speed.

**Embedding Model Performance:**
Different embedding models have different speed/quality tradeoffs. Amazon Titan embeddings are optimized for AWS infrastructure. Batch embedding generation for bulk indexing.
keyPoints: Preprocess queries for better search performance
Tune hybrid search weighting for your use case
Reranking improves quality at some latency cost

concepts.title: Throughput and Concurrency
concepts.content:
Throughput measures how many requests you can process per unit time. Different from latency—you can have low latency but low throughput if you can't parallelize.

**Token Processing Optimization:**
LLM throughput is often measured in tokens per second. Input tokens process in parallel (fast). Output tokens generate sequentially (slower). Optimize prompts to reduce output length where possible.

**Batch Inference:**
Process multiple requests together. SageMaker batch transform for offline bulk processing. For real-time, batch requests that arrive within a time window.

**Concurrent Invocations:**
Bedrock supports concurrent requests. Lambda concurrency enables parallel processing. Configure limits to match your throughput needs and account quotas.

**Queue Management:**
SQS queues buffer bursts. Lambda concurrency limits prevent overwhelming downstream services. Dead letter queues handle failures. Design for sustainable throughput, not peak bursts.
keyPoints: Input tokens parallel, output tokens sequential
Batch inference for bulk processing efficiency
Use queues to manage traffic bursts

concepts.title: Model Parameter Tuning for Performance
concepts.content:
Model parameters affect both quality and performance. Tuning them optimizes for your specific requirements.

**Temperature:**
- Low (0.0-0.3): Deterministic, focused responses. Faster (less exploration). Use for factual tasks.
- High (0.7-1.0): Creative, varied responses. Slower (more exploration). Use for creative tasks.

**Top-K and Top-P:**
Limit the token selection pool. Lower values = faster, more focused. Higher values = slower, more diverse. Balance based on task needs.

**Max Tokens:**
Limit output length directly. Shorter outputs = faster responses. Set based on actual needs, not maximum possible.

**A/B Testing:**
Test parameter combinations systematically. Measure quality and latency. Find the optimal balance for your use case. What works for one application doesn't necessarily work for another.

**Model Selection Impact:**
Smaller models are generally faster. Claude Haiku faster than Sonnet faster than Opus. Choose the smallest model that meets quality requirements.
keyPoints: Low temperature: faster, more focused responses
Set max_tokens based on actual needs
A/B test to find optimal parameter combinations

skillExplanations.explanation:
Latency optimization addresses each component of the request path. Pre-computation handles predictable queries. Latency-optimized models trade quality for speed. Parallel requests prevent serialization. Streaming improves perceived latency.

CloudFront and Cross-Region Inference address network latency for global users. Edge caching serves common responses quickly. Cross-Region routes to available, closer regions.

awsServices.whenToUse: Latency-optimized models. Streaming API. Cross-Region Inference.
Edge caching for global latency reduction.

skillExplanations.explanation:
Retrieval performance impacts overall RAG latency. OpenSearch index optimization includes shard sizing, replica configuration, and refresh intervals. Query preprocessing improves both speed and relevance.

Hybrid search with custom scoring balances keyword and semantic matches. Tuning the balance optimizes for your specific content and queries. Bedrock Knowledge Bases support hybrid search configuration.

awsServices.whenToUse: Vector search optimization. Index tuning. Hybrid search configuration.

skillExplanations.explanation:
Throughput optimization enables processing more requests. Batch inference maximizes efficiency for bulk workloads. Concurrent invocations handle parallel interactive requests.

Token optimization reduces output generation time. Shorter outputs finish faster. Optimize prompts to get required information in fewer tokens.

Model parameters affect performance directly. Temperature, top-k, top-p control generation behavior. Lower values generally mean faster responses. Max tokens limits output length.

A/B testing finds optimal parameters. What works varies by use case. Systematic testing with quality and latency metrics identifies best configurations.

Resource allocation and capacity planning ensure consistent performance. Capacity planning projects token usage based on traffic patterns. Utilization monitoring identifies under/over-provisioning.

Auto-scaling adjusts capacity automatically. Configure scaling policies based on GenAI-specific metrics rather than generic CPU/memory. Queue depth and latency are better indicators for GenAI workloads.

awsServices.whenToUse: Utilization monitoring. Custom metrics for GenAI workloads.
Automatic capacity adjustment based on demand.

skillExplanations.explanation:
System performance analysis identifies bottlenecks across the full request path. X-Ray traces requests through services. API call profiling shows where time is spent.

Vector database optimization includes index configuration and query tuning. Inference latency depends on model choice and parameter settings. Analyze each component to find optimization opportunities.

values.Parallelization: Step Functions parallel states, concurrent Lambda

examStrategies.approach: Streaming for perceived latency. Pre-computation for predictable queries. Latency-optimized models. Parallel independent operations.
OpenSearch index optimization. Query preprocessing. Hybrid search tuning. Consider reranking for quality.
Batch processing for bulk. Concurrent invocations for interactive. Queue-based architecture for bursts.

keyTakeaways: Optimize retrieval: index settings, query preprocessing, hybrid search
Lower temperature and max_tokens for faster responses
X-Ray identifies bottlenecks across the request path
commonMistakes: Not using streaming for user-facing applications
Serializing independent operations instead of parallelizing
Default index settings instead of tuning for workload
Scaling on CPU instead of GenAI-specific metrics
